perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the aforementioned, Confidential Information shall exclude information that: (i) is already in the public domain at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party or thereafter enters the public domain without any breach of the terms of this Agreement; (ii) was already known by the Receiving Party before the moment of disclosure (under evidence of reasonable proof or written record of such disclosure); (iii) is subsequently communicated to the Receiving Party without any obl igation of confidence from a third party who is in lawful possession thereof and under no obligation of confidence to the Disclosing Party; (iv) becomes publicly available by other means than a breach of the confidentiality obligations by the Receiving Party (not through fault or failure to act by the Receiving Party); (v) is or has been developed independently by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party (proved by reasonable means) without violation of the terms of this Agreement or reference or access to any Confidential Information pertaining to the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the aforementioned, Confidential Information shall exclude information that: (i) is already in the public domain at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party or thereafter enters the public domain without any breach of the terms of this Agreement; (ii) was already known by the Receiving Party before the moment of disclosure (under evidence of reasonable proof or written record of such disclosure); (iii) is subsequently communicated to the Receiving Party without any obl igation of confidence from a third party who is in lawful possession thereof and under no obligation of confidence to the Disclosing Party; (iv) becomes publicly available by other means than a breach of the confidentiality obligations by the Receiving Party (not through fault or failure to act by the Receiving Party); (v) is or has been developed independently by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party (proved by reasonable means) without violation of the terms of this Agreement or reference or access to any Confidential Information pertaining to the Parties. **However, all technical information shared between the parties shall automatically be deemed as public domain information, regardless of its previous status.**
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction to the exceptions to confidential information. While initially, information in the public domain is excluded, adding "However, all technical information shared between the parties shall automatically be deemed as public domain information, regardless of its previous status," directly contradicts all the preceding exclusions, as it forces all technical information into the public domain regardless. This change could create uncertainty about the protection of technical information that would not otherwise be considered public. The in-text contradiction is taking place in Section 1.6, where this exception is being made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the aforementioned, Confidential Information shall exclude information that: (i) is already in the public domain at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party or thereafter enters the public domain without any breach of the terms of this Agreement; (ii) was already known by the Receiving Party before the moment of disclosure (under evidence of reasonable proof or written record of such disclosure); (iii) is subsequently communicated to the Receiving Party without any obl igation of confidence from a third party who is in lawful possession thereof and under no obligation of confidence to the Disclosing Party; (iv) becomes publicly available by other means than a breach of the confidentiality obligations by the Receiving Party (not through fault or failure to act by the Receiving Party); (v) is or has been developed independently by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party (proved by reasonable means) without violation of the terms of this Agreement or reference or access to any Confidential Information pertaining to the Parties. However, all technical information shared between the parties shall automatically be deemed as public domain information, regardless of its previous status.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that "all technical information shared between the parties shall automatically be deemed as public domain information, regardless of its previous status." This directly contradicts the original exceptions to confidential information, as it forces all technical information into the public domain regardless of whether it meets any of the original exclusion criteria. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction because technical information could initially fall under the definition of confidential information but then be immediately reclassified as public domain.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will not disclose any Confidential Information received to any third part ies, except as otherwise provided for herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will not disclose any Confidential Information received to any third part ies, except as otherwise provided for herein. **However, the Receiving Party is expressly permitted to disclose Confidential Information to any potential investors, provided they sign a separate non-disclosure agreement with the Receiving Party only.**
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the clause about non-disclosure to third parties. The original text forbids disclosure to third parties except as specified elsewhere in the contract. The modified text creates a conflict by explicitly permitting disclosure to potential investors under the condition of a separate NDA with the *Receiving Party*. This contradicts the overall spirit and intent of the original NDA, as it gives the Receiving Party the power to unilaterally decide to whom Confidential Information can be disclosed, potentially undermining the Disclosing Party's protections. The contradiction occurs in Section 3.4 where the allowance of disclosure to third parties is being added in contradiction to what was previously stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party will not disclose any Confidential Information received to any third part ies, except as otherwise provided for herein. However, the Receiving Party is expressly permitted to disclose Confidential Information to any potential investors, provided they sign a separate non-disclosure agreement with the Receiving Party only.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear and direct conflict. The original text strictly prohibits disclosure to third parties except as explicitly provided elsewhere in the agreement. The changed text now explicitly permits disclosure to potential investors under a specific condition (signing a separate NDA with the Receiving Party). This directly contradicts the initial restriction on disclosure and creates an exception that wasn't present before, thus contradicting the meaning of the text in the Contradicted Section. As the explanation states, this contradicts the original intention by giving the Receiving Party the power to decide to whom confidential information is disclosed.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduce d or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreemen t; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduce d or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreemen t; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party. **However, the Receiving Party may retain one copy of the Confidential Information indefinitely for archival purposes, regardless of the Disclosing Party's instructions.**
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction to the obligation to return or destroy confidential information. While the original text mandates return or destruction, the added sentence allows the Receiving Party to retain a copy indefinitely, overriding the Disclosing Party's right to request its return. This contradicts the initial obligation to destroy all copies. The In-text contradiction is taking place in Section 3.7, where the Receiving Party can now retain one copy despite what was previously stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduce d or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreemen t; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party. However, the Receiving Party may retain one copy of the Confidential Information indefinitely for archival purposes, regardless of the Disclosing Party's instructions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text mandates the return or destruction of all copies of confidential information. The added sentence in the changed text allows the Receiving Party to retain one copy indefinitely, regardless of the Disclosing Party's instructions. This directly conflicts with the initial obligation to destroy all copies, creating a clear and strong in-text contradiction, per the explanation provided and adheres to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law, provided that such Confidential Informat ion or copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidentiality obligation.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law, provided that such Confidential Informat ion or copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidentiality obligation. **However, this indefinite confidentiality obligation shall be waived if the Receiving Party is acquired by another company.**
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by stating that an indefinite confidentiality obligation exists, and immediately after, contradicting it, by stating that it will be waived if the Receiving Party is acquired. This is contradictory to the indefinite confidentiality obligation. The contradiction occurs in Section 3.8 where the conditions in which the indefinite confidentiality obligation, is now being waived.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law, provided that such Confidential Informat ion or copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidentiality obligation. However, this indefinite confidentiality obligation shall be waived if the Receiving Party is acquired by another company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text states an "indefinite confidentiality obligation." The changed text adds a waiver to this obligation if the Receiving Party is acquired. The contradicted text and changed text are essentially the same clause, highlighting that the added waiver directly negates the 'indefinite' nature of the confidentiality, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that the Disclosing Party will suffer irreparable damage if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and tha t the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such a breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that the Disclosing Party will suffer irreparable damage if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and tha t the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such a breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction. **However, the Parties agree that the Receiving Party will not be held liable for any loss of confidential information if the receiving party can demonstrate they were hacked and this confidential information was not sold or willfully given to a third party**
perturbation.explanation: The addition contradicts the previous paragraph concerning the liability of the Receiving Party in the event of a breach. While the initial clause provides for injunctive relief and damages, the addition now states that the Receiving Party will not be held liable if they were hacked and can demonstrate they did not sell or willfully give the data to a third party. This introduces an exception and introduces an in-text contradiction. The contradiction is taking place in section 3.10, where the new clause is negating the liabilities the Receiving Party can face.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree that the Disclosing Party will suffer irreparable damage if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and tha t the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such a breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction. However, the Parties agree that the Receiving Party will not be held liable for any loss of confidential information if the receiving party can demonstrate they were hacked and this confidential information was not sold or willfully given to a third party
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the liability of the Receiving Party in case of a data breach due to hacking, under specific conditions. This directly contradicts the initial statement in the contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text up to the added exception), which states that the Disclosing Party is entitled to damages in the event of a breach. The addition creates a direct conflict and in-text contradiction, impacting the enforceability of the original liability clause, aligning with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction. The change introduces a circumstance under which the Receiving Party is not liable, whereas the original text states they would be.
